skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Routes video editing requests to the appropriate RunComfy model for restyling, background swaps, packaging changes, motion transfer, and outfit edits.
skill.summary: Route video edit prompts to RunComfy models with model-specific guidance.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no malicious prompt injection, credential theft, or hidden executable payloads. Most hits are Markdown command examples, placeholder URLs, model names, or documentation text. The skill still intentionally invokes the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and media URLs to a third-party API, and writes downloaded outputs, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party CLI Execution and Remote Video Processing
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to call the local RunComfy CLI and submit user prompts, media URLs, and generated outputs through RunComfy services. This is core functionality, not hidden malware, but it creates privacy, network, dependency, and filesystem-write exposure that users should understand before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines explicitly describe local CLI invocation, RunComfy authentication, remote API polling, output downloads, and third-party media URL handling. The behavior is intentional and documented, so confidence is high for a medium publish-with-warning risk rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections correspond to inline command names and fenced shell examples for installing or running the RunComfy CLI. They are not executable code inside the skill repository, but they document commands an agent may run when the user requests video editing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences or inline command documentation, not scripts loaded by the skill. The only real concern is the intended agent action of invoking a third-party CLI, captured separately as a medium finding.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Detections Are Placeholder URLs and Output Paths
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts come from placeholder HTTPS values such as media URLs and placeholder output directories. No evidence found of path normalization bypass, arbitrary file reads, or traversal against local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequences are visible inside example URLs and placeholders in Markdown. There is no local file access code at these lines.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Detection Documents Token Storage
low_findings.description: The hidden home-directory finding describes where the RunComfy CLI stores its token after login. The skill does not include code that reads or uploads this token, but the storage location is a sensitive operational detail.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 221 is in a Security & Privacy section and describes CLI token storage behavior. No evidence found that the skill itself accesses the file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary Markdown text, model names, or words in prompts and headings. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic APIs being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and contains no programming-language cryptography calls. The static matches are not supported by semantic context.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to a limitation sentence about avoiding total subject replacement in Lucy edits. No evidence found of host inspection, environment probing, or system inventory behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is natural-language model guidance. It does not request system information or execute reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation With User-Provided Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill routes prompts and media URLs into RunComfy CLI commands. The Markdown states the CLI sends JSON directly and does not shell-expand prompt content, which reduces injection risk, but the external command and third-party service boundary remains material.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The invocation pattern and stated input boundary are documented in the skill. The risk is bounded by the CLI behavior described, but external execution and remote data processing are confirmed.

content.user_title: Edit Videos with RunComfy Model Routing
content.value_statement: Video editing prompts often fail when routed to the wrong model. This skill selects a RunComfy video model and applies targeted prompting guidance for the requested edit.
actual_capabilities: Classifies video edit intent into restyle, background swap, packaging swap, motion transfer, or outfit edit routes.
Selects Wan 2.7 Edit-Video for general restyles, background changes, packaging swaps, and commercial polish.
Selects Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control when a reference clip should drive target character motion.
Selects Lucy Edit Restyle for lightweight identity-stable outfit, lighting, and atmosphere edits.
Provides RunComfy CLI invocation patterns with model-specific input fields and output directory handling.
Includes prompting guidance for preserving face, pose, lip movement, framing, audio, and temporal consistency.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and a valid token or login session.
Processes prompts and media URLs through RunComfy services, so sensitive media needs user review first.
Model limits still apply, including duration, resolution, input format, and aspect ratio constraints.
The skill routes to one model per call and does not combine multiple video edit models automatically.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Product Video
use_cases.description: Swap a product background or packaging design while preserving camera motion and original framing.

use_cases.title: Create Character Motion Variants
use_cases.description: Transfer motion from a reference clip to a target character while keeping character identity stable.

use_cases.title: Restyle Social Clips
use_cases.description: Change outfit, lighting, or scene atmosphere for short clips without rebuilding the edit workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Background Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple background change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this video with RunComfy. Preserve the person, pose, lip movement, and audio. Change only the background to a clean modern office.

prompt_templates.title: Product Packaging Swap
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that uses a reference image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reference image to replace the package design in this video. Keep the hands, camera motion, lighting direction, and original audio unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Motion Reference Transfer
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that maps motion from one clip to a target character.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer the dance motion from the reference clip to the target character. Keep the target identity, background style, and timing coherent.

prompt_templates.title: Route and Optimize an Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that asks the agent to choose the best RunComfy route.
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose the best RunComfy video edit model for this request. Explain the route, keep one edit direction, and preserve identity, motion, framing, and audio where possible.

output_examples.input: A talking-head clip needs a modern office background while keeping the speaker and audio unchanged.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request to Wan 2.7 Edit-Video and prioritizes face, pose, lip movement, and source audio preservation.

output_examples.input: A character image should follow the movement from a dance reference video.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request to Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control and uses the reference clip as the motion driver.

output_examples.input: A short walking clip needs a professional outfit change with the same face and movement.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request to Lucy Edit Restyle and keeps the prompt focused on one localized outfit change.

best_practices: State preservation goals before the requested change, especially for face, pose, motion, framing, and audio.
Use one edit direction per generation to reduce visual drift and model confusion.
Review source media sensitivity before sending URLs or prompts to RunComfy services.
anti_patterns: Do not combine restyle, packaging replacement, background swap, and motion transfer in one prompt.
Do not use reference images for general style edits unless direct appearance transfer is needed.
Do not assume the skill can bypass model limits for duration, resolution, file size, or aspect ratio.

faq.question: Does this skill edit videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI to send jobs to RunComfy remote model services.

faq.question: Which models can it route to?
faq.answer: It routes to Wan 2.7 Edit-Video, Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control, or Lucy Edit Restyle.

faq.question: Can it preserve original audio?
faq.answer: Yes, for supported routes. The skill includes guidance for preserving source audio when the model supports it.

faq.question: Can it handle private media?
faq.answer: Use caution. Media URLs and prompts are processed by RunComfy services, so sensitive content needs review first.

faq.question: Does it support long videos?
faq.answer: Only within each model limit. Some routes are limited to short clips or specific duration ranges.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for external CLI execution, network processing, and output downloads.
